fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer physical therapist license to alabama, physical therapist compact alabama, alabama physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Alabama Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address: Alabama Board of Physical Therapy, P.O. Box 241361, Montgomery, AL 36124-1361

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Alabama.

steps.title: Register on the Online Services Portal
steps.description: Create an account on the Alabama Board of Physical Therapy Online Services portal (alabamainteractive.org/pt/) by clicking 'First Time User? Register Now.' This is the first step for all applicants.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Alabama Board of Physical Therapy Online Services portal and complete the online application for 'Licensure by Endorsement (Reciprocity)' for Physical Therapists.

steps.title: Request Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your accredited physical therapy program to the Alabama Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Verify Licensure in Other States
steps.description: Request official verification of current and past licenses from all states/jurisdictions where you have ever held a PT license. This verification must be sent directly to the Alabama Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Submit to a criminal background check via fingerprinting. Detailed instructions will be provided by the Board after the online application is submitted.

steps.title: Submit Additional Documentation
steps.description: Ensure all other required supporting documents, as outlined in your online application checklist or by the Board, are submitted directly to the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alabama physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $150. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alabama participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Alabama without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 8 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Alabama
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Alabama, applicants must register and complete the application through the Alabama Board of Physical Therapy Online Services portal.
